body.title: [sprint] /sorted: ask intention open-ended, then map it against the extant intention list
body.description:
**Problem:** /sorted Step 2.6 Q1 ('Which intention does this serve?') can be presented to Akien as a menu of pre-written options. That is backwards: Akien does not pick an intention from a list. The correct shape is (a) ask the human their intention OPEN-ENDED (free text, no menu), then (b) the skill itself reads the EXTANT intentions and reports how the new one fits — advances / competes with / merges into / decomposes an existing intention. Intentions are living entities (see project_intentions_as_living_entities); a new one must be situated against the current set, not captured in isolation.

**Proposed shape:** In skills/sorted/SKILL.md Step 2.6, change Q1 to an open-ended ask (no option list), and ADD an explicit sub-step after the human answers: 'Examine the stated intention against the extant intention list — report fit (advances/competes/merges/decomposes) and link the related intention(s).' Store the mapping in the decision body alongside the Intention section.

**Open question (flag, do not guess):** there is no dedicated intentions/ store dir today; intentions currently live embedded in decisions/ and architecture/ intention-points. Until a dedicated intentions store exists, the 'extant intention list' = grep over devlab/runtime/memory/{decisions,architecture} for kind/Intention sections. Note this source explicitly in the skill so it is not silently wrong.

**Affected files:** skills/sorted/SKILL.md (Step 2.6 only).
**Design rules:** skills live in repo skills/ (canonical, not ~/.claude/skills) — feedback_skills_in_repo, feedback_no_skills_sync_symlink.
**Scope boundary:** edit only Step 2.6 of the sorted skill; do not touch other steps or other skills. No code changes.
**Test plan:** no automated test (skill-doc edit). Proof = next /sorted run asks Q1 open-ended and emits an intention-fit line in the decision JSON. Close shipped-unproven if proof must wait for a live /sorted run, naming the missing lever (skill-doc behavior has no red-test form).

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
**Completion criteria:** skills/sorted/SKILL.md Step 2.6 Q1 is open-ended (no option menu) AND a new explicit sub-step instructs the skill to map the stated intention against the extant intention list (source: grep over devlab/runtime/memory/{decisions,architecture}) and record the fit line in the decision JSON. Verified by a live /sorted run emitting an intention-fit line; close shipped-unproven naming the no-red-test-form lever if proof must wait.
body.intention: I intend that every new intention captured by /sorted is situated against the intentions that already exist, so intentions evolve as living entities rather than accumulating in isolation.
